title: Monsoon is here: Why you're paying out of pocket for sudden illnesses despite having health insurance
summary: Health insurance covers major hospitalisation costs, but many medical expenses, such as doctor consultations and medicines, are often paid out of pocket. Experts say financial preparedness and understanding policy coverage can help families avoid unexpected healthcare costs.

title: SIP returns disappointing? Here's when to stay invested and when to switch funds
summary: Negative or muted SIP returns in the initial years are not uncommon, especially for equity mutual funds. Experts say investors should judge a fund over a full market cycle and switch only if there are signs of structural issues.

title: NRI tax filing: Moved abroad? Here's when you still need to file an ITR in India
summary: Moving abroad does not end your tax obligations in India. From excess TDS and capital losses to property sales and future tax planning, here's when NRIs should file an ITR.

title: PFRDA launches NPS PRIDE-Disha: How the new tool helps you choose the right pension fund
summary: NPS PRIDE-Disha lets subscribers compare pension funds using XIRR based on regular contributions instead of only point-to-point returns. The platform uses historical NAV data since 2008 to help investors make more informed fund selection and switching decisions.

title: ITR filing 2026: How to access interest certificate from SBI, HDFC Bank, ICICI Bank, PNB and India Post
summary: The ITR due date is near and taxpayers are urged to gather important documents, including interest certificates, to ensure accurate reporting of income. Here is a step-by-step guide to download interest certificate from SBI, HDFC Bank, ICICI Bank, PNB and India Post.

title: Automatic EPF transfer after job change? Avoid these 6 mistakes to prevent delays
summary: EPFO has introduced automatic PF transfers for eligible members. However, the facility works only if certain conditions are met.

title: Sensex down 9.37% in 2026: Should investors wait or stay invested? Here's what history shows
summary: The Sensex has fallen 9.37% so far in 2026, reviving concerns over whether investors should wait for markets to recover. Four decades of historical data offer insight into how Indian equities have behaved during major corrections.

title: No large-cap fund reached Sharpe ratio of 1; several mid and small caps delivered stronger risk-adjusted returns
summary: After analysing the list of large-cap funds, no fund was found to have touched a Sharpe ratio of 1. Among the large-cap funds, WhiteOak Capital Large Cap Fund recorded the highest Sharpe ratio at 0.64.&nbsp;

title: Can GST officers issue a fraud notice without proof? Madras HC explains
summary: The Madras High Court has ruled that GST officers need not conclusively establish fraud before issuing a notice under Section 74 of the CGST Act. The judgment clarifies the legal threshold for invoking fraud proceedings under the GST law.

title: AI and robots can replace coders, but not nurses, labourers or teachers: Report finds jobs costliest to automate
summary: A June 2026 report by Planera found that replacing workers in professions such as nursing, construction and home care with AI and robots would cost employers several times as much as paying wages to human workers.

title: HDB Financial Q1 Results: Profit jumps 38% YoY to Rs 785 crore; NII up 20%
summary: HDB Financial Q1 Results: Net interest income rose 20% to Rs 2,509 crore from Rs 2,092 crore a year earlier. Net total income increased 17% to Rs 3,185 crore from Rs 2,726 crore in the year-ago quarter.

title: HDFC AMC Q1 Results: Net profit rises 12% to Rs 837 crore, revenue up 14%
summary: HDFC Asset Management Company reported a 12% rise in Q1 FY27 net profit to ₹837 crore, supported by higher revenue and asset growth. Quarterly average AUM climbed 13% year-on-year, while shares gained over 2% after the earnings announcement.

title: Rupee ends flat as merchant bids, oil blunt comfort from modest inflows
summary: The Indian rupee hovered close to a one-month low on Wednesday, driven by modest portfolio inflows and increased corporate demand for dollars. title: Global Market: Foreign investors add $132 billion to US securities in May as equity demand stays strong
summary: According to Reuters, the Treasury's monthly Treasury International Capital (TIC) report showed that net long-term purchases by foreign investors reached $1.33 trillion over the 12 months through May, reflecting sustained overseas demand for U.S. assets.

title: 'We faltered, did not move quickly:' How IBM CEO Arvind Krishna's statement led to $70 billion wipeout
summary: IBM shares rebounded nearly 2% in premarket trade after plunging 25% in their worst single-day fall in 58 years, wiping out nearly $70 billion in market value. title: Global Market: ASML raises 2026 outlook as AI boom drives strong earnings, plans capacity expansion
